titleStmt.title: SII 27.379: original edition by M. D. Sampath, H. K. Narasimhamurthy, Madhav N. Katti and B. R. Gopal
No. 379. (A.R. No. 483 of 1928-29.) SURIYA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.

summary.p: Pedestal of the Nandi in front of the Sadāśiva temple.

This record in characters of about the 15th century, is dated the cyclic year Piṅgala, Mithuna 17, Sunday. It states that the image of Nandikēśvara was set up in the temple at Sureya by Nārāyaṇa Sēnabōva, son of Saṅkuvadhikāri of Nūje, in the reign of Vīra Narasiṅga-Lakshmapparasa alias Baṅgarāja Voḍeya. As this chief was ruling in the latter half of the 15th century, the cyclic year mentioned in the record may be equated with 1497 A.D. But the other details are irregular as the given tithi corresponded to June 14 of that year when the weekday was Wednesday.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
piṁgala saṁvatsarada mithuna msa 17 ne ravivāradallū śrīmatu vīranarasiṁga lakṣmapparsarāda baṁgarāja voḍēra kāladalli nūjeya
saṁkuvadhikārimaga nārāyaṇa sēnabōvanu sureyada śrīdēvara saṁnidhiyalli pratiṣṭemāḍida naṁdikēśvaranu naḍasida saṁbugaiddu śubhamastu śrī .
Digital edition of SII 27.379 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
